spec_match.rationale: It exposes a real port chooser and terminal to write Arduino commands to a selected serial port using real navigator.serial APIs.

interactive.rationale: Includes a terminal where inputs can be written to a real port with an active event listener that continuously reads and logs responses from the selected device.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns well with the blurb, illustrating a field workflow (Android + BT REPL) with microcontrollers.

edge_cases.rationale: Gracefully logs cancellation/disconnections, release of reader/writer locks, and absence of serial APIs.

references.rationale: Correctly links to ChromeStatus entry 5139978918821888 and related concepts.

design_system.rationale: Conforms with the design system styling, responsive layout, and has sufficient color contrast.

summary: A fantastic, highly practical terminal/REPL for sending commands to an Arduino over Bluetooth serial, leveraging real web serial reader/writer loops.
